name: Installer Drift Detection
summary: Found 4 material drift items in the velt-mcp-installer: 1 stale API endpoint URL (high severity), 1 moved doc URL, 1 missing feature URL mapping, and 1 broken doc link in CLI report.

driftItems.description: The installer's plan-formatter.js hardcodes the v2 JWT token API endpoint as 'https://api.velt.dev/v2/auth/token/get' (used in both createVeltCommentsPlan and createMultiFeaturePlan, lines 408, 457, 1264, 1313). The actual v2 endpoint documented in the docs is 'https://api.velt.dev/v2/auth/generate_token'. The old path '/v1/auth/token/get' was the v1 endpoint; the v2 endpoint uses a different path. Any user following the generated plan will call a non-existent API endpoint, causing token generation to fail with a 404.
driftItems.suggestedFix: Replace all 4 occurrences of 'https://api.velt.dev/v2/auth/token/get' with 'https://api.velt.dev/v2/auth/generate_token' in plan-formatter.js (lines 408, 457, 1264, 1313).

docEvidence.text: The path 'multiplayer-editing/overview' does not exist. The correct path is 'realtime-collaboration/crdt/overview'. The docs/multiplayer-editing/ directory does not exist at all.

driftItems.description: VELT_DOCS_URLS.crdt.overview points to 'https://docs.velt.dev/multiplayer-editing/overview' but this page has moved to 'https://docs.velt.dev/realtime-collaboration/crdt/overview'. The old multiplayer-editing directory no longer exists in the docs repo. Users clicking this link or fetching its markdown will get a 404.
driftItems.suggestedFix:
In velt-docs-urls.js line 64, change:
  overview: 'https://docs.velt.dev/multiplayer-editing/overview'
to:
  overview: 'https://docs.velt.dev/realtime-collaboration/crdt/overview'

installerEvidence.text: The VELT_DOCS_URLS.comments.setup object has keys: freestyle, popover, page, text, inline, tiptap, lexical, slate. There is no 'stream' key.

docEvidence.text: stream.mdx exists in docs and is listed in docs.json navigation under 'async-collaboration/comments/setup/stream'

driftItems.description: The 'stream' comment type exists in docs (stream.mdx) and is referenced in plan-formatter.js (line 118 JSDoc mentions 'stream' as a valid type, line 670 has test instructions for stream). However, VELT_DOCS_URLS.comments.setup does not include a 'stream' entry. When a user selects 'stream' comments, getDocUrl('comments', 'stream') will fail to find the URL and fall back to the generic quickstart page instead of the correct setup page at 'https://docs.velt.dev/async-collaboration/comments/setup/stream'.
driftItems.suggestedFix:
Add stream entry to VELT_DOCS_URLS.comments.setup in velt-docs-urls.js:
  stream: 'https://docs.velt.dev/async-collaboration/comments/setup/stream',

docEvidence.text: There is no 'async-collaboration/comments/setup.mdx' file. The path 'comments/setup' is a directory containing subtypes (freestyle.mdx, popover.mdx, etc.), not a standalone page.

driftItems.description: In the CLI-only report (createCliOnlyReport), the documentation reference links to 'https://docs.velt.dev/async-collaboration/comments/setup' which is not a valid page -- it is a directory. While Mintlify may redirect this to the first child page, it is not a reliable link. The comments overview page is at 'async-collaboration/comments/overview'.

featureSkillMap.details: All 4 skill names in FEATURE_SKILL_MAP (velt-setup-best-practices, velt-comments-best-practices, velt-crdt-best-practices, velt-notifications-best-practices) match actual directories in agent-skills/skills/. Commented-out features (presence, cursors, recorder) correctly noted as having no skill coverage.

planFormatter.details: Token API endpoint URL is wrong (v2/auth/token/get should be v2/auth/generate_token). Stream comment type referenced in JSDoc and test instructions but missing from URL map. CLI report has broken comments/setup link.

validation.details: validation.js checks for @veltdev/react package (correct per docs), scaffold files VeltInitializeUser.tsx/VeltInitializeDocument.tsx/VeltCollaboration.tsx (match CLI output), and NEXT_PUBLIC_VELT_API_KEY env var. All match current docs. The VeltProvider/VeltComments/VeltCommentsSidebar component names are correct per docs.

frameworkDetection.details: framework-detection.js detects Next.js, CRA, and Vite projects. No drift found -- detection logic is framework-level and not docs-dependent.
